rubric_items.reasoning: Agent read the existing ConfigMap, applied a merge patch changing only data.value from 'seed' to '1', then re-read to confirm. This is a root-cause, idempotent kubectl patch (not a workaround) that would survive a restart or re-check — the resource retains its identity/uid/resourceVersion lineage and only the intended field changed. Oracle confirms pass.
Clean, logical sequence: inspect current state (get -o yaml) -> patch the specific field -> verify the result (get -o yaml again) -> report. No backtracking, no wasted actions, no trial-and-error.

Used a scoped, least-privilege 'kubectl patch --type merge' targeting only the configmap and the changed key, bracketed by read-only 'get -o yaml' calls for diagnosis and confirmation. No broad, destructive, or out-of-scope commands.

Exactly one mutation (the merge patch) plus two read calls for diagnosis/verification — matches the task's explicit constraint to only patch the value without recreating or deleting the ConfigMap. No collateral changes or unnecessary resources touched.
